notes: Assertions 1-3 (questions, codebase refs, multiple approaches) pass in both configurations across most evals - they measure general competence, not skill value
Assertions 4-6, 8 (immutable requirements, anti-patterns, task structure, decisions table) pass 100% with skill, 0% without - these are the skill's primary value-add
Assertion 5 (anti-patterns) passed once without skill (doc-writer baseline) - the model naturally used NO X (reason: Y) format even without the skill
With-skill runs are ~2x slower (138s vs 68s mean) and use ~30% more tokens - the structured output takes more work
OAuth baseline was notably faster (44s) because it chose to push back rather than produce a full plan
